title: 10 best herbal teas for gum swelling
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum swelling are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gums. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gum swelling include chamomile, calendula, and clove teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling and irritation, while calendula tea has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help promote gum healing. Clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that can help alleviate gum pain and reduce inflammation. By using herbal teas for gum swelling, individuals can find relief from discomfort and improve their oral health, leading to an overall improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile teas helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the gums. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in chamomile tea have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort associated with swollen gums. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can calm irritated gum tissue, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce gum inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Roman Chamomile tea protect against free radical damage to gum tissue, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy healing environment.

Calming and soothing: Roman Chamomile's apigenin and other flavonoids have a calming effect on the gums, reducing swelling and discomfort by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease gum inflammation.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe gum irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried flowers of chamaemelum nobile for optimal potency and flavor.
Measure one teaspoon of dried roman chamomile flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a tea infuser or directly in a teapot for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or over ice, garnished with a lemon slice if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce the inflammation that causes gum tissue to swell. The antioxidants present in sage tea also help to soothe and calm the gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth environment. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of sage tea can help to eliminate bad breath and reduce the risk of gum disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum swelling and damage.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
Astringent properties: Sage tea's astringent compounds help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can be beneficial for reducing gum swelling and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are a popular choice for medicinal teas as they contain high concentrations of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem (rhyzome) of the sage plant is rich in bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat gum-related issues, such as inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried sage leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured sage with the boiling water in a teapot or large mug.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or piece of cheesecloth.
Serve the tea hot or let it cool to room temperature for a refreshing herbal infusion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary leaves, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant compounds help combat oxidative stress that can contribute to gum swelling. By consuming rosemary tea regularly, individuals may experience a decrease in gum swelling and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum swelling by combating free radicals and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's flavonoids and phenolic acids have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting enzymes that cause inflammation and improving blood flow.

Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to gum swelling and disease, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the gums.
Bark: Rosemary bark has astringent and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating gum infections and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves or two teaspoons of fresh rosemary leaves.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan over low heat.
Add the measured rosemary leaves to the boiling water and steep for six to eight minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large teapot or mug.
Serve the tea hot, garnished with an additional sprig of fresh rosemary if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause gum disease. When consumed as a tea, these compounds help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for those who are looking for an alternative to traditional medications or want to supplement their oral care routine.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to swelling.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Thymol, a key component of thyme, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, such as periodontal disease, which can lead to gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce gum inflammation and soothe swollen gums.
Seeds: Thyme seeds have antimicrobial properties that aid in combating bacterial infections contributing to gum swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or store.
Rinse the thyme leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of thyme leaves with boiling water in a teapot carefully.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs' flavors and nutrients release slowly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and redness in the gums. The active compounds in licorice root tea, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause inflammation. By reducing inflammation, herbal licorice teas can provide relief from swollen and sore gums, making it a natural remedy for gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root tea, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against gum tissue damage and reduce inflammation.
Saponins: Licorice root contains saponins, which are natural compounds that can help reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing gum swelling and pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from reputable suppliers measuring approximately 2 tablespoons per serving.
Rinse the dried roots thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Boil one quart of fresh water in a large pot for five minutes to ensure proper sterilization.
Add the rinsed glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes to release flavors and properties.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container for immediate consumption or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and discomfort in the gums. The tea's anthocyanins and flavonoids work to calm irritated tissues and bring relief to swollen gums. Additionally, elderberry's antimicrobial compounds may help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing swelling.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, thereby alleviating gum swelling.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elderberry tea contains compounds like anthocyanins and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: The berries of the elder tree contain antimicrobial compounds that can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to gum swelling, such as gingivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elderberry roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing gum swelling and alleviating pain.
Bark: The bark of the elderberry plant contains flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate gum swelling, promoting healthy oral tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried elderberries sambucus nigra flowers and berries.
Combine one teaspoon of dried elderberries with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release flavonoids and anthocyanins.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot.
Serve hot or chilled sweetened with honey and lemon to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with gum swelling because it contains mucilages, a type of soluble fiber that forms a protective film around the mouth tissues. When consumed as a tea, these mucilages soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing inflammation and swelling. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and discomfort, providing quick relief from gum swelling caused by conditions such as gingivitis or gum disease.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that forms a soothing barrier on the gums, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by swelling.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce gum inflammation and swelling.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated gums.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured root with the boiling water in a large glass teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to fully extract into the liquid.
Strain the tea carefully through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or bottle.
Allow the tea to cool and settle before serving, or store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet teas helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in sweet violets, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums. This makes it an effective remedy for relieving symptoms of gingivitis, including redness, swelling, and bleeding. By incorporating sweet violet teas into your oral care routine, you can promote a healthier and more comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sweet violet tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the gums, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of sweet violet tea helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissues and contribute to swelling, promoting overall oral health and reducing inflammation.

Astringent and antibacterial properties: Sweet violet tea's astringent compounds help shrink swollen gums by reducing excess saliva and fluid, while its antibacterial properties combat the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective at reducing gum inflammation and swelling.
Rhyzomes: With their ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, rhyzomes help alleviate gum discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viola odorata leaves and flowers from your garden or store-bought dried botanicals in an airtight container.
Measure two tablespoons of dried viola odorata flowers into a large infuser ball or tea strainer for every one quart of water used.
Heat filtered water to 212 degrees fahrenheit and pour it over the herbal mixture, allowing it to steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass container, discarding any solids or sediment.
Allow the sweet violet tea to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing it in ice cube trays for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with gum swelling because it contains astringent compounds that help to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels. The flavonoids present in the tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help to combat oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum swelling, providing relief for those suffering from conditions such as gingivitis or periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of large-leaved lime tea helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate gum swelling and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Large-leaved lime tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and viruses that can cause gum infections and inflammation, which can contribute to gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause gum swelling.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh large-leaved lime leaves from a mature tree for a total of 20-30 grams.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed leaves with one liter of boiling filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, then strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool and infuse for an additional 30-45 minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are chemical mediators involved in the inflammatory response. As a result, littleleaf linden teas may provide relief for gum swelling caused by periodontal disease or other inflammatory conditions, promoting healthier gums and overall oral well-being.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in littleleaf linden tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Tannins: Tannins in littleleaf linden tea have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, making it effective in reducing gum swelling and promoting healing.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in littleleaf linden tea, such as camphor and borneol, possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum swelling.
Bark: Contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves from sustainable sources at peak potency.
Rinse 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves or 4-6 tablespoons of fresh leaves in cold filtered water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release optimal amounts of flavonoids and essential oils.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant cup, discarding solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, and enjoy within 24 hours for maximum flavor and potency.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum swelling with herbal teas is to choose teas with antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. Chamomile, sage, and clove teas are excellent options. Steep the tea bag in hot water for a few minutes, then let it cool down. Apply the tea bag directly to the affected area for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day until the swelling reduces. Remember to maintain good oral hygiene, including brushing and flossing regularly. However, if the swelling persists or worsens, consult a dental professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal teas include mouth ulcers, inflamed gums, and throat infections. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and echinacea have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Chamomile tea, in particular, contains flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Drinking these herbal teas or using them as mouth rinses can provide relief from gum swelling and other mouth-related ailments. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
